message:
bench(api): add value_and_grad shared-forward-pass vs separate-calls benchmarks

The recent value_and_grad unification (e72afd0) merged the forward pass
so that value and gradient are computed in a single traversal rather than
two independent jit+grad calls. This commit adds criterion benchmarks to
quantify that improvement and catch regressions.

New benchmark group "value_and_grad_runtime" with four scenarios:
- shared/square_plus_linear: value_and_grad on a simple program
- separate/square_plus_linear: jit + grad called independently
- shared/deep_100_nodes: value_and_grad on a synthetically deep Jaxpr
  (100 chained x^2+1 stages, 200 equations total)
- separate/deep_100_nodes: jit + grad independently on the same deep Jaxpr

The deep Jaxpr is constructed via build_deep_value_and_grad_jaxpr() which
chains Mul(x,x) -> Add(_,1.0) nodes to stress the forward/backward pass
sharing. This exercises the AD tape at scale and makes the shared-vs-separate
cost difference clearly visible in benchmark output.

Also renumbers existing benchmark section comments (3->4, 4->5) to
accommodate the new section 3, and registers the new group in the
criterion_group! macro.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
